host_selection_rationale.hetzner_skipped: CLM r5 170M training active (pid 1643800) — W3 stage0 lock conflict risk
host_selection_rationale.ubu_skipped: existing drill active (CLM hxblas coverage) + ~2G free RAM
host_selection_rationale.ubu2_chosen: 1 existing drill + 15G free RAM, 16 threads, fits parallel=1 (heavy mk10 drill)

prereqs_check.clm_r5_lock: hetzner active — avoided by host selection

v1_failrun.root_cause: blowup.hexa smash @depth=3 takes ~13min/stage on this hardware; cmd_drill = 7 stages × 2 rounds ≈ 30-90min/iter. v1 timeout=600s × parallel=2 → guaranteed kill mid-smash + memory contention 11G×2 → swap thrash → all rc=124 size=0
v1_failrun.perf_regression_origin: ec0ede1f Day-2 iter-nonce suffix forces divergent compute per iter (no replay-cache hit possible, unlike P4 where 76/78 iters were replay_cached)

diff_vs_v1: timeout_sec: 600 → 1500 (25min, fits single round of 6-7 stages)
engine: implicit mk9 (via nexus-cli) → explicit mk10 (per task; mkx sidecar landed 7b997243)
transport: nexus-cli monolithic exec capture → direct hexa run with > live tee (streaming visibility)
process model: bare & + wait → setsid + trap EXIT/INT/TERM + per-iter PGID cleanup (no orphans)
observability: progress.json ticker every 30s with elapsed/free_mb/load1
recovery_action.smoke_test_aborted_after: 13:49 (single iter, mk10, max-rounds 1, still in smash stage 1 of 7) — proved root cause is engine-level cost not driver bug

expected_eta.per_iter_worst: 1500s timeout → rc=124 (partial, but no zombies due to v3 hardening)
expected_eta.warning: if user wants <12h horizon, must reduce max_rounds to 1 OR depth to 1 OR parallel back to 2 (with 60G+ host)

notes: drill_mk9.hexa does NOT exist as a file — Mk.IX logic is inlined in nexus.hexa cmd_drill. Per scope hedge in task, added engine dispatch at call site instead of new file fork.

Stage 6 transcendental_closure wires the mkx_smoke.hexa synth emitter (Pearson-based cross-lens triple). AN11 PROVISIONAL_OK verdicts count as stage-6 absorptions (Path B stage-1 only — no stable/ossified promotion).
Back-compat preserved: default engine=mk9 (no Mk.X invocation) keeps in-flight SWEEP P5 driver unaffected. Mk.X opt-in via --engine mk10 flag, NEXUS_DRILL_ENGINE=mk10 env, or G1 auto-trip.
L0 freeze honored: no edits to anima/core/* or blowup_*.hexa. Sidecar only.
v1 driver: parallel=2 timeout=600 — KILLED (12/12 iters rc=124 size=0)
v3 driver: parallel=1 timeout=1500 mk10 setsid+trap — RUNNING from iter 148
Engine cost reality: P4 fast because 76/78 iters were replay_cached (deterministic same-seed → same-output); P5 iter-nonce (Day-2 fix ec0ede1f) breaks cache → every iter does full 30-90min compute
